message.content:
<teammate-message teammate_id="steve-schoger" color="pink" summary="NEW change needed: fg-muted gray-500 to gray-600, one line">
Lea, yes -- the original 10 adjustments are all confirmed and approved. Great work on those.

But there's a NEW change I need from Heydon's contrast audit (which came in after those 10). Did you see my message about `--color-fg-muted`?

Line 212 currently reads:
```css
--color-fg-muted: var(--color-gray-500);  /* secondary text (~4.6:1 vs white...) */
```

It needs to change to:
```css
--color-fg-muted: var(--color-gray-600);  /* secondary text (~7.4:1 vs white) */
```

Heydon found that gray-500 at 4.6:1 is borderline -- too tight for text at font-size-sm. Gray-600 gives us comfortable headroom at 7.4:1. This is a design token governance change that I'm approving.

This is a one-line change. Please make it and commit with a message like "Bump --color-fg-muted to gray-600 for WCAG AA contrast safety."
</teammate-message>

content.content:
210→    --color-bg-raised:     white;                  /* cards, raised surfaces */
   211→    --color-fg:            var(--color-gray-800);  /* primary text */
   212→    --color-fg-muted:      var(--color-gray-600);  /* secondary text (~7.4:1 vs white -- comfortable AA for all text sizes) */
   213→    --color-fg-subtle:     var(--color-gray-400);  /* placeholders, hint text (3:1 -- NOT for body text) */
   214→    --color-border:        var(--color-gray-200);  /* default borders */

<system-reminder>
Whenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.
</system-reminder>

file.content:
--color-bg-raised:     white;                  /* cards, raised surfaces */
    --color-fg:            var(--color-gray-800);  /* primary text */
    --color-fg-muted:      var(--color-gray-600);  /* secondary text (~7.4:1 vs white -- comfortable AA for all text sizes) */
    --color-fg-subtle:     var(--color-gray-400);  /* placeholders, hint text (3:1 -- NOT for body text) */
    --color-border:        var(--color-gray-200);  /* default borders */

input.content:
Steve, I just re-read line 212 directly from the file. It already says:

```
--color-fg-muted: var(--color-gray-600);  /* secondary text (~7.4:1 vs white -- comfortable AA for all text sizes) */
```

It's `gray-600`, not `gray-500`. The change you're requesting is already in place. You may be looking at a cached or older version of the file. Please run `git pull` and check again.
